items.headline: Edin Terzic to become Athletic Bilbao coach next season
items.description: Former Borussia Dortmund boss Edin Terzic will take charge of Athletic Bilbao next season, the club announced on Tuesday, following the departure...

items.headline: Thousands at funeral for Italian auto champ and Paralympic gold medalist Alex Zanardi
items.description:
PADUA, Italy (AP) — Thousands of fans have attended the funeral for Alex Zanardi, the Italian auto racing champion-turned-Paralympic gold medalist who died last week at 59.
Zanardi’s handbike was placed near the altar inside the basilica in Padua where the service was held on Tuesday, and his hand cycling teammates sat in the front row near his wife, Daniela, and son, Niccolò.

items.headline: Hurricanes overcome penalties, deficit to beat Flyers in OT for 2-0 lead in 2nd-round playoff series
items.description:
RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Rick Tocchet got the response he wanted from his Philadelphia Flyers. Rod Brind'Amour had reason to be frustrated with his Carolina Hurricanes taking too many penalties, disrupting their preferred 5-on-5 rhythm.
And yet, the Hurricanes are still unbeaten in the NHL playoffs, thanks to a comeback from their first deficit of the postseason and a gritty-effort overtime winner from Taylor Hall.

items.headline: Stars captain Jamie Benn again has a decision to make about whether to play another season
items.description:
FRISCO, Texas (AP) — Dallas Stars captain Jamie Benn again goes into the offseason with a decision to make about whether to come back for another NHL season.
His coach wants him to keep playing, and so does longtime teammate Tyler Seguin. Benn, who will turn 37 on July 18, sounds as though he will take some time to ponder an 18th season — all in Dallas — and another shot at a Stanley Cup title.

items.headline: After the CBA opened the floodgates, some notable WNBA All-Stars decided to play closer to home
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — All-Stars Brittney Griner, Nneka Ogwumike, Skylar Diggins and Satou Sabally all found new places to play this season, taking advantage of free agency. Angel Reese has a new home after she was traded to Atlanta.
This might have been the most movement by All-Stars during an offseason in WNBA history. Not that it comes as a huge surprise. More than 80% of the players in the league were free agents after last season, knowing a huge pay day awaited with the new collective bargaining agreement that was signed in March.
